commands.review_summary:
This PR wraps MiniMax music streaming SSE frame parsing in a try/catch and skips malformed JSON frames.

PR surface: Source +5. Total +5 across 1 file.

Reproducibility: yes. by source inspection: a `text/event-stream` MiniMax music response containing `data: not-json` reaches the bare `JSON.parse` on current main. I did not run a local repro because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Manual review is appropriate because the remaining blocker is contributor real behavior proof and maintainer merge gating, not an autonomous repair task.

target.title: fix(minimax): guard music generation SSE JSON.parse against malformed frames
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

`extensions/minimax/music-generation-provider.ts:266` calls `JSON.parse(json)` on raw SSE `data:` frames without try/catch. A malformed frame from the MiniMax music streaming endpoint causes an unhandled `SyntaxError` that propagates through `readStreamingTrack` and crashes the music generation.

## Changes

- `extensions/minimax/music-generation-provider.ts`: wrap `JSON.parse(json)` in try/catch → `continue` on failure (skip malformed frame, stream continues)

**Scope**: `readStreamingTrack` SSE frame parsing only (1 line). Note: line 408 `JSON.parse` is already inside a `try {}` block.

Label: bugfix | merge-risk: 🟢 minimal | AI-assisted

## Evidence

### Existing tests continue to pass

```
pnpm exec vitest run extensions/minimax/music-generation-provider.test.ts

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
```

### Before/after

```
// Before
const frame = JSON.parse(json) as MinimaxMusicStreamFrame;  // 💥 SyntaxError

// After
let frame: MinimaxMusicStreamFrame;
try { frame = JSON.parse(json) as MinimaxMusicStreamFrame; } catch { continue; }
// Malformed frame → skipped, stream continues
```
target.author: lsr911
